questions.question: Which of the following operating systems is NOT typically used on desktop or laptop computers?
The desktop background is also known as:
What does the shortcut key Ctrl + S do in most software applications?
questions.option_2: Searches for text in a document

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for managing shortcut links and desktop visual layout?
Which of the following actions will permanently delete a file from an MS Windows system, bypassing the Recycle Bin?
questions.option_1: Selecting the file and pressing Shift + Delete keys
questions.option_3: Right-clicking on the file and selecting 'Delete'
questions.option_4: Dragging the file to the Recycle Bin

questions.question: Which key combination is commonly used in Windows OS to open the Task Manager?
What does the abbreviation 'SRAM' stand for in computer memory?
Which of the following is considered a poor file management practice on a computer system?
questions.option_1: Creating separate folders for documents, images and videos
questions.option_2: Using nested folders to organise large sets of data
questions.option_3: Giving files meaningful and descriptive names
questions.option_4: Saving temporary files in unrelated and scattered folders

questions.question: Which type of memory loses all its stored data when the power is turned off?
Which of the following allows users to delete a file using the mouse?
questions.option_2: Drag it to the Recycle Bin

questions.question: What is the main function of RAM in a computer?
questions.option_2: To provide temporary storage for running programs

questions.question: To change the desktop wallpaper, which option would you choose?
Which of the following is primarily a system configuration tool rather than a direct visual element of the MS Windows desktop interface?
Which of the following devices is primarily used to convert digital signals into sound for audio output?
A computer system mainly includes which three components?
Which of the following is an example of an operating system commonly used on laptops?
Which of the following is a characteristic of primary memory in a computer?
questions.option_2: It holds data temporarily for quick access during processing.
questions.option_3: It is slower than secondary memory.
questions.option_4: It is used to back up files externally.

questions.question: Which of the following is a small hand-held pointing device that controls the two-dimensional movement of the cursor on a screen?
Which desktop element in Windows displays system notifications, date and time, and background apps status?
What happens when you create a folder with the same name in the same location?
Which of the following types of memory is volatile, meaning its contents are lost when the computer is turned off?
Which tool in Windows helps one manage and organize files and folders?
What happens when you delete a file or folder using the Delete key?
questions.option_2: The file is copied to another folder.
questions.option_3: The file is sent to the Recycle Bin.
questions.option_4: The file is permanently deleted without warning.

questions.question: What happens when a user renames a file in MS Windows?
questions.option_1: The file is converted into a different format.
questions.option_2: The file's name changes, but its content remains the same.
questions.option_3: The file is deleted and a new file is created.
questions.option_4: The file is moved to a different location.
